channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trudeau says Americans are realizing that Trump's tariffs on Canada make life a lot more expensive
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau says Americans “are beginning to wake up to the reality that tariffs on everything from Canada would make life a lot more expensive.”.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes hit Gaza and also target suspected weapons sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli strikes killed at least six people in the central Gaza Strip, Palestinian medical officials said Monday, while also hitting suspected chemical and long-range weapons sites in Syria to keep them from rebels who seized Damascus. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hungary's Orbán meets with Trump and Musk
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán met Monday with President-elect Trump, his incoming national security adviser and billionaire Elon Musk, who has wielded significant influence in Trump's orbit. "USA today. The future has begun!" Orbán posted on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: As Israel advances on a Syrian buffer zone, it sees peril and opportunity
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The dramatic downfall of Syrian President Bashar Assad presents possible danger, and an opening, for neighboring Israel. After fighting wars on multiple fronts for months, Israel is now concerned that unrest in Syria could spill over into its territory.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Incoming Trump envoy presses Syrian rebels to release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming hostage affairs envoy Adam Boehler pressed the rebels in Syria to release the American hostages in the country as the region grapples with more instability.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel deploys paratroopers to Syria in 'defense activities' after fall of Assad
item.description: Israeli forces released footage of their deployment into Syria following the fall of Bashar Assad's regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian community in Chicago celebrates end of Assad regime
item.description: It was a sea of green flags on Michigan Avenue Sunday as hundreds of Chicagoans celebrated the end of the regime in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Illness delays trial of men charged with cutting down Britain's scenic Sycamore Gap tree
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The trial of two men charged with cutting down the majestic Sycamore Gap tree last year along Hadrian’s Wall in northern England was postponed until January because one defendant was ill, a judge said Monday. Justice Christina Lambert adjourned the case until the week of Jan.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea imposes a travel ban on President Yoon over martial law declaration
item.description: The South Korean Justice Ministry imposed an overseas travel ban against President Yoon Suk Yeol on Monday over his martial law declaration last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Landslides and flash floods hit Indonesia’s Java island, leaving 10 dead and 2 missing
item.description: Indonesian rescuers have recovered 10 bodies that were swept away in flash floods or buried under tons of mud and rocks that hit hilly villages on the country’s main island of Java.
item.dc:creator: Niniek Karmini And Rangga Firmansyah, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tonga's prime minister quits moments ahead of no-confidence motion in parliament
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Tonga’s Prime Minister Siaosi Sovaleni abruptly resigned in parliament on Monday ahead of a planned no-confidence vote in his leadership, capping a period of fraught relations between his government and Tonga’s king.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The rise and fall of Bashar and Asma Assad
item.description: Syrian President Bashar Assad and his wife Asma started as a couple that could have broken the country's tyrant regime, though the opposite happened.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's Justice Ministry imposes a travel ban on President Yoon over martial law
item.description: South Korea’s Justice Ministry has imposed an overseas travel ban on President Yoon Suk Yeol as authorities investigate allegations of rebellion and other charges in connection with his short-lived declaration of martial law last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Assad granted asylum in Russia, arrives in Moscow
item.description: Syrian President Bashar Assad and his family have been granted asylum in Russia and arrived in Moscow on Sunday after the Syrian government fell.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow after fall of Damascus, Russian state media say
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad fled to Moscow and received asylum from his longtime ally, Russian media said Sunday, hours after a stunning rebel advance seized control of Damascus and ended his family’s 50 years of iron rule.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, SARAH EL DEEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls for immediate ceasefire in Ukraine and says a US withdrawal from NATO is possible
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump on Sunday pushed Russian leader Vladimir Putin to act to reach an immediate ceasefire with Ukraine, describing it as part of his active efforts as president-elect to end the war despite being weeks from taking office.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How it happened: Two seismic weeks that toppled Syria's government
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Half a century of rule by the Assad family in Syria crumbled with astonishing speed after insurgents burst out of a rebel-held enclave and converged on the capital, Damascus, taking city after city in a matter of days.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turner says he believes there will be 'assessment as to whether' troops in Syria 'should' stay under Trump
item.description: House Intelligence Committee Chair Mike Turner (R-Ohio) said Sunday that under President-elect Trump, he believes there will “be an assessment as to whether or not” troops currently in Syria “should” stay.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: US will remain in eastern Syria and seek to prevent IS resurgence, Pentagon says
item.description: Hours after Syrian rebels announced they had toppled Bashar al-Assad's government, the U.S. deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East said the U.S. will remain in eastern Syria to prevent an ISIS resurgence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian dictator Bashar Assad flees into exile as Islamist rebels conquer country
item.description: A lightning Syrian Islamist offensive over just days contributed to the demise of the over 50-year reign of the Assad Baathist regime in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's president avoids an impeachment attempt over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol avoided an opposition-led attempt to impeach him over his short-lived imposition of martial law, as most ruling party lawmakers boycotted a parliamentary vote Saturday to deny a two-thirds majority needed to suspend his presid
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's President Yoon survives impeachment attempt after his party boycotts vote
item.description: South Korean President Yoon survived an impeachment motion after members of his party boycotted the vote. Yoon attempted to impose martial law earlier this week, sparking backlash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral reopens its doors for first time since devastating 2019 fire
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against 'Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: As part of an investigation into the ‘Ndrangheta mafia in the city of Brescia, Italian police have arrested 25 people including a nun known for her prison work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alternative healer gets 10 years in UK prison for death of woman at slap therapy workshop
item.description: Hongchi Xiao, a 61-year-old alternative healer who used 'slapping therapy' to treat maladies, has been sentenced to 10 years in prison for the death of a 71-year-old woman.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: United Kingdom cracks down on junk food TV advertisements
item.description: The United Kingdom's Department of Health estimates that 7.2 billion calories will be removed from the diets of British children.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean lawmakers support suspending president’s powers after short-lived martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean lawmakers are calling for President Yoon Suk Yeol's constitutional powers to be stripped after his short-lived martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada bans more assault firearms, suggests donating guns to Ukraine
item.description: Top leaders in Canada announced that they had banned an additional 324 assault-style firearms in a continued effort for more stringent gun control.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actress dies after consuming amphibian's venom at spiritual cleansing retreat
item.description: Mexican actress Marcela Alcázar Rodríguez, 33, died after ingesting Amazonian frog venom during a cleansing ritual at a spiritual retreat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes on a Gaza tent camp kill at least 21 people, hospital says
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes tore through a tent camp for displaced Palestinians in southern Gaza on Wednesday, sparking fires and killing at least 21 people, according to the head of a nearby hospital, in the latest assault on a sprawling tent city that Israel designated a huma
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US 'seriously concerned' by developments in South Korea
item.description: The United States was surprised by South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol’s brief declaration of emergency martial law.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean parliament votes to defy president by lifting his declaration of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law late Tuesday, vowing to eliminate “anti-state” forces as he struggles against an opposition that controls the country’s parliament and that he accuses of sympathizing with communist North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9
